seoTitle: Install n8n Locally on Windows or Mac with npm
metaDescription: Learn how to install n8n locally on Windows or Mac using npm, from setting up Node.js to launching your first local workflow dashboard.
intro: Running n8n locally is a good option if you want to build and test automations before committing to a hosted or Docker-based setup. This guide covers installing the required Node.js prerequisites on both Windows and Mac, using npm to install n8n itself, and getting your local dashboard up and running.

steps.heading: Install Node.js
steps.body: Download and install the correct version of Node.js for your operating system, then verify it in your terminal or PowerShell console.

steps.heading: Verify npm is working
steps.body: Open your terminal and check your Node and npm versions to confirm the installation succeeded before continuing.

steps.heading: Install n8n globally with npm
steps.body: Run the standard npm global install command to download the official n8n package onto your machine.

steps.heading: Launch the local server
steps.body: Start n8n from the command line to initialize its local storage directories and default port configuration.

steps.heading: Open the dashboard in your browser
steps.body: Navigate to your local instance address in a web browser to reach the n8n interface.

steps.heading: Complete the owner setup
steps.body: Finish the onboarding wizard to create your admin login credentials before building your first workflow.

faq.question: Do I need Docker to install n8n locally?
faq.answer: No, this method uses npm and Node.js directly, so Docker isn't required, though it's an alternative approach many people also use.

faq.question: Which Node.js version should I use?
faq.answer: You should use a version compatible with your n8n release, which you can confirm by checking the current n8n documentation before installing.

faq.question: Will my data persist if I restart my computer?
faq.answer: Yes, n8n stores workflow data and credentials in a local directory that persists across reboots unless you manually delete it.

faq.question: Can I access my local n8n instance from another device?
faq.answer: By default a local install is only reachable from the same machine unless you configure additional network settings, which is outside the scope of a basic local setup.
